use_when: A product, offer, or place must be understood in the first viewport
A marketing story needs mechanism and proof
patterns.responsive: Keep the product signal first; stack proof after the primary action and leave the next section visible.

use_when: The content is research, a case study, or a long argument
Source trust and reading rhythm matter
patterns.responsive: Collapse rails into inline evidence blocks and preserve readable measure.

use_when: Two approaches, states, or products need direct comparison
Tradeoffs matter more than a single recommendation
patterns.responsive: Stack by criterion rather than placing two long columns sequentially.

use_when: Users repeat actions against selected objects
Context and tools must remain visible
patterns.responsive: Turn secondary panes into drawers or routes while preserving the active object and primary action.

use_when: Users monitor several related signals
Attention and action need prioritization
patterns.responsive: Order by decision priority; convert wide comparisons to scroll-bounded tables or focused views.

use_when: A small number of measures lead to detailed records
Overview must explain what to inspect next
patterns.responsive: Keep definitions and units with each metric; collapse detail into progressive disclosure.

use_when: Events, phases, or changes over time are central
Sequence and causality matter
patterns.responsive: Use one vertical sequence and keep labels adjacent to events.

use_when: Users browse many comparable items
Filtering and return context are important
patterns.responsive: Move filters into a persistent drawer trigger and preserve result count and active filters.

use_when: A system, workflow, or dependency path must be explained
Branches and ownership matter
patterns.responsive: Offer a readable linear fallback or bounded pan/zoom with keyboard controls.

use_when: A product workflow spans several screens or modes
Navigation and state continuity must be tested
patterns.responsive: Adapt navigation to the target platform and keep the primary task reachable in one transition.

use_when: Visual evidence carries the story
A portfolio, launch, or transformation needs paced reveals
patterns.responsive: Preserve subject visibility and replace fragile overlays with ordered content when space is limited.

use_when: The browser is the presentation runtime
A narrative must advance frame by frame
patterns.responsive: Maintain the accepted frame ratio; provide overview and readable fallback outside full-screen mode.

use_when: A component system or interaction family is the deliverable
States and variants need side-by-side review
patterns.responsive: Keep controls accessible and let previews resize without changing the component state.

use_when: A repository needs an immediate product and audience signal
Real product output must support a concise positioning claim
patterns.responsive: Preserve identity and one proof at thumbnail width; remove secondary labels before shrinking the primary proof.

use_when: One message must carry a launch, campaign, event, or product statement
The final artifact is a fixed-format still
patterns.responsive: Redesign hierarchy for each aspect ratio and keep critical text inside the declared safe area.

use_when: A product workflow must be understood through time
A short motion asset needs a complete action-to-result arc
patterns.responsive: Keep each scene independently legible and define a static poster that carries the core claim without motion.

use_when: Procedural SVG, Canvas, WebGL, or motion is the primary visual material
A fixed stage needs deterministic rendering
patterns.responsive: Use explicit stage ratios and recompose controls or explanatory text outside the fixed visual field.

use_when: A high-impact visual task needs three materially different directions
A manager must compare structure, type, color, interaction, or motion before implementation
patterns.responsive: Keep previews comparable at one geometry; stack complete directions rather than shrinking them below readable size.

use_when: An app flow must be reviewed in its target device context
Several screens or platform variants need direct comparison
patterns.responsive: Preserve device aspect ratio and move secondary screens into a scrollable strip or route.

use_when: A presentation has more than ten slides
Slides need isolated CSS, parallel production, or editable HTML-to-PPTX conversion
patterns.responsive: Keep slide geometry fixed and scale the stage; provide an overview rather than reflowing individual slides.

use_when: Voice timing drives a long-form visual explanation
Music, effects, and motion must form one continuous narrative
patterns.responsive: Use one deterministic master timeline and preserve a readable poster or transcript fallback.

use_when: Data, a process, or a comparison must be understood as one fixed visual
A sourced claim needs an annotated explanation rather than a dashboard
patterns.responsive: Recompose hierarchy for each target ratio; never shrink a desktop canvas until labels become unreadable.
